title: rudbeckia hirta var. floridana
intro: Rudbeckia hirta var. floridana, also known as Florida Black-Eyed Susan, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the southeastern United States. The medicinal properties of Rudbeckia hirta var. floridana are attributed to its flowers, leaves, and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues, among others. Herbal preparations of Rudbeckia hirta var. floridana include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
